diagrams.title: DFD Components Symbols
diagrams.description: The four standard symbols used in DFD notation
diagrams.purpose: Learn standard DFD notation and symbols used globally

diagrams.title: Context Diagram Example
diagrams.description: Level 0 DFD showing an Order Processing System with external entities like Customer, Warehouse, and Accounts
elements: External entities: Customer, Warehouse, Accounts Department
Data flows: Order, Order Confirmation, Invoice, Shipping Details
diagrams.purpose: Understand how to draw and interpret the highest-level DFD

diagrams.title: Level 1 DFD Decomposition
diagrams.description: Decomposed view of order processing showing major sub-processes
elements: Processes: 1.0 Receive Order, 2.0 Process Payment, 3.0 Update Inventory, 4.0 Arrange Shipping
Data Stores: Customer Database, Product Database, Order Records
Data flows connecting processes to stores and entities
diagrams.purpose: Learn how to decompose a parent process into detailed child processes

diagrams.title: DFD Balancing Example
diagrams.description: Illustration showing how parent process inputs/outputs match child diagram
elements: Child: Detailed diagram with same 2 inputs entering, same 2 outputs exiting
diagrams.purpose: Understand the concept of balancing between DFD levels

visuals.title: Flow Oriented Modeling
visuals.description: Visual diagram for flow oriented modeling
